fields.icd_conflict_detail: All three documents (referral form, clinical notes, and prescription) consistently list ICD-10 code G12.21 (Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis). No conflict was detected across any documents.
fields.priority_reason: Patient has a confirmed diagnosis of Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), a progressive and severe neurological condition, which classifies this case as high clinical risk requiring urgent equipment fulfillment.
fields.notes: 1) Authorization Ref # is missing from the referral form — adjuster Diana Cruz should be contacted at (860) 555-0143 or d.cruz@hartford.com to obtain prior authorization before delivery. 2) The clinical context raises a concern: ALS is a progressive neurodegenerative disease and not a typical workplace injury diagnosis; the injury date of 01/24/2026 and workers' comp claim framing may warrant clinical/adjuster clarification on whether the ALS diagnosis is injury-related or a pre-existing condition being managed under this claim. 3) Patient height/weight is 5'1" / 219 lbs — confirm the standard manual wheelchair (E1161) weight capacity is sufficient; a bariatric or heavy-duty frame may be more appropriate depending on the specific chair selected. 4) The prescription is valid through 05/11/2026 (90 days from 02/11/2026). 5) No interpreter or language requirement noted.

fired_rules.source: CMS DME MAC Jurisdiction D — Prior Authorization Requirements
fired_rules.message: Workers compensation claim — authorization reference number required for E0143 billing. Missing auth ref blocks claim submission.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Dear Diana Cruz,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding a durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your patient, **Eric Roberts**, under claim number **WC-2026-084465**. We are unable to advance processing of this referral until the following outstanding items have been received:

**Required – Hard Block (Routing on Hold):**
- **Authorization Reference Number (auth_ref):** This item is preventing routing and must be resolved before the referral can move forward.

**Also Required:**
- **Preferred Language (language):** Please provide the patient's preferred language so we may ensure appropriate communication and documentation standards are met.

To avoid delays in care for Mr. Roberts, we kindly request that the above information be submitted **within 24 business hours** of receipt of this notice.

Please reply directly to this email or contact our intake team through your standard communication channel. If you have any questions or believe this information has already been submitted, please don't hesitate to reach out so we can resolve this promptly.

Thank you for your attention to this matter and your continued partnership.

Warm regards,
**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME | Intake Department
